steps.title: Identify the Grammatical Structures
steps.content: Before the blank: incomplete appositive noun phrase (“a record label that was a pioneer of early hip hop”). After the blank: independent clause starting with the subject “Chambers.”

steps.title: Apply the Appositive Punctuation Rule
steps.content:
Appositives in the middle of a sentence must be enclosed by commas on both sides to mark their boundaries.

The appositive needs “music” to complete the noun phrase, and then requires a closing comma before the main clause resumes.

The correct answer provides this comma; the others either omit it, misuse a semicolon, or introduce an awkward relative clause.

That confirms A is correct.
